note: publish V13 RC1 public artifact metadata and SHA256SUMS paths. (A) website/api/v13_rc1_artifact_manifest.json — new public-safe mirror of the operator-local V13 RC1 artifact bundle MANIFEST.json (sost-v13-rc1-artifact-manifest-public/v0.1). Exposes bundle_id v13rc1bundle-fe3b041de40a3f62, rc_id v13-rc1, activation_height 12000, min_commit d604aafafd65dcb90c34fa5845bedee8038daffc, the SHA-256 + size_bytes of the three binaries (sost-node 18.3 MB sha c5441d7d..., sost-miner 6.7 MB sha 5b65cfb2..., sost-cli 5.8 MB sha b18f5ec0...), the three configs + two preflight reports + the deterministic tarball (10.8 MB sha c0345dce...), the 11 const-true safety flags, and a release_status field locked to 'metadata_only_not_signed_not_uploaded'. Explicitly OMITS the operator-local fields (repo_root_basename, no_copy_binaries_mode, preflight_was_ready) from the internal manifest. (B) website/api/v13_rc1_SHA256SUMS.txt — verbatim 3-line copy of the bundle's SHA256SUMS so any reviewer can `sha256sum -c` against binaries they later download from a separate manual operator release-publication step. (C) docs/V13_RC1_PUBLIC_ARTIFACT_METADATA.md — 6-section explainer: what this is, what it is NOT, how to verify once binaries are downloaded, why a separate public manifest, the release_status state machine (metadata_only_not_signed_not_uploaded -> signed_metadata_only -> signed_and_published, every transition manual operator action only), the const-true safety contract, and pointers. (D) website/sost-explorer.html — added a new amber side-tile 'PUBLIC ARTIFACT METADATA (binaries NOT here)' next to the existing PUBLIC RC MANIFEST tile in the V13 RC1 operator banner, and a new amber-bordered paragraph stating 'Public V13 RC1 artifact metadata is live ... binaries themselves are NOT committed to this repo and the SHA256SUMS file is NOT yet signed ... release_status = metadata_only_not_signed_not_uploaded'. (E) website/sost-protocol-spec.html — extended the compact V13 RC1 status block path-reference line with a second line for the artifact-metadata file paths. (F) website/api/explorer_version.json bumped v267 -> v268. Pure HTML/text/JSON edit. ZERO binary content committed. ZERO src/, ZERO schemas, ZERO scripts, ZERO tests, ZERO consensus, ZERO wallet/payment/broadcast/release-upload changes. NO signature published — signing remains explicit manual operator action and a corresponding *.sig file will appear next to SHA256SUMS only after that step lands (out of scope here). The on-chain V13 hardfork itself does not depend on any of these files; the website surface is published purely so anyone reading the BitcoinTalk thread can cross-check binary hashes before installing whatever the operator's eventual release announcement points to.
